summary: Battle-hardened tournament over-achievers under Zlatko Dalić — a World Cup final (2018) and semi-final (2022) on their recent CV, even after a disappointing Euro 2024. Built, as ever, on a robust, experienced midfield, now flying the flag through 40-year-old Luka Modrić.
style: Shifts between a 4-2-3-1 and a 3-4-3 with similar principles — control the tempo (70% average possession in qualifying, behind only Spain, Portugal, Germany and England). Young midfielder Petar Sučić (Inter) drops into a situational back three to free the full-backs/wing-backs high, rotating with Modrić to stitch the attack together.
strengths: Elite midfield control and game management — 70% possession in qualifying
Deep tournament experience; rarely overawed, and suited to the searing heat
A blend of veterans (Modrić, Kramarić, Perišić) and emerging talent (Sučić, Baturina)
weaknesses: An ageing squad (avg age 29, among the oldest European qualifiers)
Midfield depth thinned by retirements (Brozović, Rakitić) and Kovačić's injury return
Press can be improved; can be open to counters

keyPlayers.name: Luka Modrić
keyPlayers.note: Ballon d'Or winner playing in a fifth World Cup at 40 — Croatia's oldest-ever major-tournament player, joining Ronaldo (41) and Džeko (40) as rare 40-something outfielders; still showing no signs of stopping (91% of AC Milan's minutes, more than any of his Real Madrid campaigns)

keyPlayers.name: Petar Sučić
keyPlayers.note: 22-year-old taking the baton — drops into the back line or pushes forward to balance old and new

tactics: Control possession and tempo, drop a midfielder to build through a back three, and lean on unmatched experience to manage games — a style well-suited to the heat. The questions are legs over a long run and a midfield in transition between generations.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group L (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
